title: Summit Optimization: Enchanted Rock Ascent
description: A childhood field experiment in vertical problem solving.
story: This project focused on testing the limits of my early climbing abilities during an ascent of Enchanted Rock in Texas. By carefully navigating granite slopes and optimizing step placement, I demonstrated an emerging capacity for perseverance and altitude acquisition. The experience laid the groundwork for future adventures that required determination, curiosity, and the willingness to climb a little higher just to see what was there.

title: Grassroots Campaign Engineering: Choir Presidency
description: An eighth-grade campaign powered by strategic messaging and canine charisma.
story: This leadership project involved designing and executing a highly successful campaign for middle school choir president. The strategy centered around an innovative marketing asset: my dog, Archimedes, whose campaign appearances dramatically improved voter engagement. By combining humor, visual branding, and enthusiastic outreach, the campaign secured victory and launched my first official leadership role.

title: Pi Day Celebration Initiative
description: A multidisciplinary event merging mathematics, desserts, and industrial history.
story: This project explored the intersection of mathematical culture and historical storytelling through the creation of a large-scale Pi Day celebration. Attendees enjoyed pie while learning that March 14 also marks the patent date for Eli Whitney’s cotton gin. The result was an event that demonstrated how creative presentation can transform historical trivia into memorable shared experiences.

title: Applied Dairy Humor: Cheese Wheel Performance
description: A theatrical experiment in costume design and high-density pun delivery.
story: This performance project involved inhabiting a large wheel-of-cheese costume for a theatrical production built around an escalating series of cheese-related puns. The challenge combined costume engineering, comedic timing, and commitment to the bit. The outcome proved that immersive character design—especially when cheese-shaped—can significantly amplify audience laughter.

title: Cold Climate Structural Engineering
description: A first encounter with snow leading to experimental frozen architecture.
story: When an unusual winter storm delivered significant snowfall to Texas, I seized the opportunity to prototype my first large-scale snow structure. The result was a remarkably cubic snow construction, demonstrating rapid adaptation to unfamiliar materials and weather conditions. This project highlighted creativity under unusual environmental constraints and the joy of building something unexpected.

title: University Admission Breakthrough
description: A long-term preparation initiative culminating in acceptance to MIT.
story: This milestone project represented the culmination of years of academic preparation, curiosity, and persistence. Acceptance to MIT marked a turning point that would shape the trajectory of the next four years. The experience reinforced the value of pursuing ambitious goals and embracing opportunities for growth in both technical and creative fields.

title: Chorallaries Leadership & Performance Program
description: A multi-year initiative in a cappella performance and organizational leadership.
story: Beginning in 2022, I joined the MIT Chorallaries and later served as both business manager and president. This project combined artistic performance with operational leadership, helping coordinate concerts, manage logistics, and perform at major MIT events such as Convocation and Commencement. The experience demonstrated how music, teamwork, and organization can create memorable moments for an entire community.

title: International Teaching Initiative
description: A cross-cultural educational experience in an Israeli youth village.
story: This project involved traveling to Israel to teach and mentor students in a youth village community. The experience emphasized adaptability, communication across cultures, and the importance of education in empowering young people. It was both a personal and professional growth opportunity that expanded my perspective on teaching and service.

title: Hallstatt Engagement & Wedding Planning System
description: A life milestone launched in the Austrian Alps and followed by complex planning logistics.
story: This project began with an engagement in the breathtaking lakeside town of Hallstatt, Austria. The next phase involves designing and coordinating the many moving pieces of a wedding—effectively a large-scale event management challenge. The project is ongoing and combines strategic planning, collaboration, and excitement about the future.
